GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "How Seafolly revolutionised bikini shopping for Australian women", "As we look forward to the iconic Aussie swimwear brand's new Summer 2020 collection, we look back at the way it changed the way we buy swimwear altogether."', "On Bondi Beach in the 1980s, just about every cool girl was wearing a Seafolly bikini. At a time when Australia was emerging as an international tourist destination, the family-owned swimwear brand's tropical prints and high-cut Lycra suits became a beacon of our cheeky yet effortless seaside (or poolside, or riverside) style. But unlike many brands that peaked in the '80s, Seafolly's original essence has endured. Today, the brand's bathers are practically a prerequisite to any summer holiday. And again, unlike other brands, being caught on a wave in the same Seafolly suit as someone else doesn't feel like an awkward faux pas. Usually, it inspires a compliment to the tune of 'cute suit!' and a chance to meet another member of this saltwater-loving, swimwear-obsessed community. Of course, this year hasn't provided us with ample opportunities to justify a new bikini purchase. But now that summer is upon us and Australia is blooming with the promise of adventure, we've got every excuse under the blazing sun to press purchase on a new underwater outfit (or three. Or four.) And Seafolly is our first point of call - after a year spent in isolation, the brand's empowering Summer 2020 collection is exactly what we want to step out in. From sporty one-shoulder styles in juicy green to this pair of gingham high-waisted briefs - which sound vintage but look modern - this is a collection defined by deliciously bold colours and unique prints that celebrate the feminine spirit unique to Australian women. In true Seafolly style, the collection is made up of 'separates'. You might not know it, but the brand was one of the first swimwear labels to sell bikini tops and bottoms separately, which we take for granted today but was a revolutionary step forward for women because let's face it, whose butt is the same size as their boobs? And indeed, matching a strapless top in this shade with a string bottom in that shade is one of our favourite parts of the Seafolly shopping experience. It's a fun exercise in styling. But Seafolly's 'separates' model also allows women to choose pieces that fit and flatter all of their features. Putting the swimsuits in the Summer 2020 collection to the test are Aussie supermodels Charlee Fraser and Georgia Fowler, who are quite literally glowing in the brand's new season campaign. Just like Kristy Hinze did in 2001 and Miranda Kerr did in 2007, this year, Fowler and Fraser are evolving the legacy of what it means to be a home-grown face of Seafolly today - strong, active and empowered, whether you're wearing a one piece, bikini, tankini or some breezy resort wear. Seafolly might be the brand we all turn to for stylish new swimmers today. But before it became Australia's go-to bathing suit brand it transformed the way Aussie women - and women worldwide - shopped for swimwear altogether. This summer, when we go to pick out our new top and bottom, we'll be reminding ourselves of that."
